description: Characters step into a seemingly ordinary place and get trapped in escalating rituals, curses, or predatory game rules.
Romance told through bruised conversations, life detours, and the quiet physics of attachment.
Agents and assassins hunt targets across missions that spiral from intel to personal payback.
Crime plots treated like a playground—buddy dynamics, absurdity, and punchlines that keep escalating.
Animated family adventures where friendship and empathy win, often with gleeful villains and showy musical storytelling.
plain_label: Whimsical family animation with heart & villains

description: Big, grimy science-fiction worlds where survival depends on fast action, alien contact, and broken systems.
recommended_label: Doomed Horizons: Sci‑Fi Action & Alien Menace

description: Family-friendly fantasy adventures built from classic fairy-tale logic—magic, quests, and good-versus-evil tension.
plain_label: Fairy-Tale Fantasy Quest for the Young

description: War films that grip you with true-story weight and battlefield immediacy—fear, duty, and the cost of survival.
Superhero action built around shared universes—powers, secret identities, and escalating team-or-crossover stakes.
Lighthearted, fast-cut action-comedy sequels where buddy energy and villain plotting drive the laughs.
plain_label: Quippy Sequel Buddy-Action Comedy for Families

description: Stylish, morally slippery crime stories where investigation and violence braid together into a tightening web of suspicion.
Relationship-driven comedies that play out flirtation, mishaps, and heartfelt growth with sharp timing and big feelings.
Smart, funny love stories where pregnancy and growing up collide with family mess and sudden adulthood.
Brutal, landscape-heavy survival tales built from real events—where faith, brutality, and endurance grind together.
Dating comedies where friends turn into competitors and attraction becomes a high-stakes prank.
Superhero action powered by secret identities, multiversal/legacy stakes, and the constant pressure of saving a city while your life fractures.
High-speed crime stories where car culture meets police pressure, revenge plots, and big, reckless set pieces.
Serious crime stories where violence, institutions, and personal trauma corrode everyone in the frame.
plain_label: Boston crime dramas with moral rot

description: Supernatural terror that targets families—often through a child, a home, and a slow, escalating dread.
Speculative futures where robots and AI chase meaning—through loneliness, love, and the ethics of becoming human.
Movies where relationships—especially family—collide with talent, identity, and life choices, mixing comedy with bruised sincerity.
plain_label: Character-driven dramedies about family and ambition

description: High-energy comedy-crime where mismatched cops (or cop-adjacent losers) stumble into investigations with punchlines and mayhem.
plain_label: Buddy cop action comedies and parody crimes

description: Psychological thrillers where a missing person or hidden double life detonates inside intimate relationships.
Ensemble capers where a mastermind crew outsmarts rivals, regulators, and each other using misdirection and investigation.
Animated or live-action family fantasies that turn classic fairy tales into big, singable kingdoms of magic and romance.
Creature-feature terror set in claustrophobic ocean environments, where survival depends on grit, luck, and a shrinking air supply.
Horror films where teenagers or groups run the gauntlet of a killer, often spiked with revenge, dark humor, and rule-breaking supernatural menace.
Whimsical family comedies where a single parent’s life gets hijacked—usually by a nanny or stand-in caregiver—and everything spirals into heartfelt chaos.
High-intensity war films built on real missions—snipers, raids, and special-ops operations—driven by suspense and moral pressure.
Swashbuckling globe-trotting adventures where archaeologists and treasure hunters chase riddles, relics, and curses across exotic ruins and seas.
Sharp, music-friendly high-school comedies where social status games and teenage crushes collide with coming-of-age awkwardness.
Future-shock action thrillers where artificial intelligence, cyborg bodies, and brutal systems turn survival into a philosophy fight.
recommended_label: Cyber Dystopia Action (AI, Cyborgs, Futures Gone Wrong)

description: High-speed policing with mismatched chemistry, where the job gets done and the punches come with punchlines.
Bright, fast-moving animated worlds where a charming misfit (often a villain) turns into an unlikely hero for the whole family.
Sweat-soaked fight dramas where mentorship, discipline, and heartbreak build toward the next bell.
Fairy-tale worlds—sometimes familiar, sometimes flipped—where magic, queens, hunts, and wonder collide in grand adventure.
Teen and young-adult romance stories built for emotional impact—secrets, fate, and heartbreak delivered with big, heartfelt sincerity.
High-stakes voyages where astronauts (and scientists) collide with time-bending mysteries, alien signals, and the physics of survival.
Tense cat-and-mouse crime stories where a killer’s plan unspools across transit hubs and crowded cities.
plain_label: Urban hitman thrillers (revenge on the move)

description: Unsettling horror where relationships fray into paranoia, and fear spreads like a contagion through households and trust.
Bright, family-friendly animated follow-ups packed with new villains, mid-film momentum, and comedy-forward adventure set pieces.
Post-apocalyptic horror where outbreaks, conspiracies, and mutated threats force characters into desperate survival missions.
Young heroes step into magic-soaked worlds where destiny, quests, and old-school wonder collide.
Big divine premises meet fast jokes—faith, ego, and love get scrambled in a comedic afterlife of consequences.
High-energy expeditions where paleontology, DNA tinkering, and island thrills turn into full-on dinosaur mayhem.
Tense space-bound hunts where deadly extraterrestrial creatures turn every firefight into a survival horror sprint.
Big ensemble superhero adventures where stakes balloon fast and set-piece action is built for maximum crowd payoff.
Bright, mid-30s romantic chaos where love, identity, and family plans collide—often with an unexpected pregnancy or parenthood question.
plain_label: Rom-coms with a baby-shaped plot twist

description: Thoughtful, stylish romance stories where people fall into—then analyze—their feelings while cities and time do the emotional choreography.
Razor-paced action spy yarns where the central trick is infiltration—disguises, false identities, and secret agencies turning every plan into a new problem.
plain_label: Spy missions built on fake identities

description: Intense character studies of ambition and self-destruction, where music culture and addiction turn everyday life into a high-volume tragedy.
Fast, funny romance stories where chance encounters in big-city life spiral into feelings—often with weddings, friends, and public embarrassment as the accelerant.
Tense, immersive World War II stories that track soldiers through fear, chaos, and moral pressure in real-time battle conditions.
Supernatural horror built around haunting entities—often through dolls, spirits, and escalation from omen to possession.
Fast-talking, action-forward spy stories where the mission is mostly a setup for absurdity, pratfalls, and escalating criminal chaos.
plain_label: Spy parody and slapstick crime capers

description: Dark crime thrillers that obsess over investigators and predators, where identity fractures under the weight of serial murder.
Big-screen mutant superhero adventures where powers, identity, and team politics collide in high-stakes action set pieces.
When the skyline becomes a boxing ring, giant creatures duke it out in loud, high-stakes spectacle.
Tight, suspenseful stories where time travel and future technology turn every decision into a trap.
High-velocity crime action where revenge and assassin professionalism power the set pieces—often amplified in sequels.
A modern Western mood where cartels, borders, and brutality squeeze characters into irreversible choices.
Christmas chaos where family trouble and home/holiday trespass turn into slapstick action set pieces.
Animated journeys where friendship and found-family power a bright, emotionally safe adventure.
Biographical, based-on-real-events dramas where institutions, law, and power collide under pressure.
Big-screen sci-fi action with aliens, robots, and franchise-scale set pieces—built for momentum over subtlety.
Crime stories with bite—often revenge-driven—where menace and dark humor share the same spotlight.
A YA fantasy school-and-stakes run where growing up at a magical academy turns into a fight for survival.
Vampire wars and slayer myth meets hard-edged action in rain-soaked, gothic cityscapes.
A serial-killer’s ruleset turns captivity into escalating puzzles where survival is the only answer.
Leashed by politics and deadlines, a world-traveling agent turns espionage into nonstop pursuit and retaliation.
When the universe shows up, the only plan is to survive the creature and the unknown rules it brings.
A fractured identity sparks a chase through assassins, agencies, and conspiracies where every lead comes with a flashback cost.
Big-planet mythmaking where Jedi ideals collide with empires, prophecy, and high-stakes space battles.
Planet-scale calamity—natural or extraterrestrial—where survival becomes a countdown and big heroes try to out-run the inevitable.
Horror built on foreknowledge—characters glimpse their deaths and race (usually too late) to dodge a supernatural timetable.
Rowdy, risk-light comedy sequels where friend groups spiral through weddings, road trips, and workplace mayhem for maximum laughs.
Young protagonists navigate brutal futures where survival is gamified—then pivot into revolution.
Big-ego friendships and bad-timing dates collide with office life, turning awkward romance into high-volume punchlines.
Heroic warriors, mythic creatures, and biblical/ancient legends fuel thunderous battles and stylized mythmaking.
Horror that knows the rules—then weaponizes them—using meta jokes, serial-killer setups, and franchise-baiting sequels.
Stylish, tense crime stories—gangsters, detectives, and hustlers—built from momentum, moral rot, and razor-edged dialogue.
Superhero myths staged as big, conflicted battles—where identities, powers, and alliances fracture under moral intensity.
Supernatural horror where the threat feels immediate—ghosts, demons, and possessions creeping in through ordinary spaces and escalating set pieces.
Teen romance driven by supernatural creatures—vampires, werewolves, and fate—where the emotional stakes are as sharp as the supernatural ones.
High-velocity spy and hitman thrillers where intelligence agencies, revenge missions, and shootout set pieces collide.
Romantic stories for younger audiences that borrow fairytale or gothic mood—often with real-world awkwardness, scandal, or a faintly unsettling edge.
recommended_label: Teen romance in gothic-ish modern fairytales

description: Emotional coming-of-age stories where friendship, school life, family ties, and identity shifts carry the weight.
plain_label: Coming-of-age with friendship at the center

description: No-nonsense detectives and airborne chaos collide in big-city set pieces with holiday heat turned up.
Dread blooms in familiar neighborhoods as clowns, ghosts, and haunted spaces weaponize childhood fear.
Martial arts heroes fight world-ending threats with magic, alternate realities, and video-game swagger.
When retreat turns into a trap, storms, ships, and remote shorelines turn ordinary people into victims of paranoia and violence.
High-emotion biographical stories where ambition collides with family, institutions, and the mess of real-world consequences.
A breathtaking journey where survival and awe collide with family bonds, grief, and spiritual questions.
High-velocity sci-fi where alien or post-apocalyptic futures come with memory glitches, escape plans, and identity trouble.
A sharp comic spiral where everyday tech and social awkwardness turn into real-time psychological danger.
Animated stories powered by friendship, mischievous villains, and big-hearted emotional payoff—often with a pet or toy sidekick.
Broad, fast, sometimes heartfelt comedy about fathers and blended-family life colliding with responsibility, weddings, and parenting mishaps.
Big-screen fairy tales retold as live-action musical spectacles—romance, curses, and showstopping enchantments included.
High-octane espionage where aliases, infiltration, and secret agencies drive a relentless mission-chase.

description: World War II dramas built on real events—tight historical perspective, moral stakes, and the machinery of survival.

description: Raucous high-school coming-of-age stories where romance, embarrassment, and sexual misadventures fuel the jokes.
Surreal science-fiction horrors where alien or unknown threats turn dread into an atmospheric, psychological trap.

description: A lone operator with a hidden past moves through crime, war-zone grit, and tight rescue missions to settle scores with surgical brutality.
Crime unspools as fallout—abuse, grief, and moral debt—pulling police, courts, and families into a slow-motion reckoning.
Big-hearted animated quests where music, magic, and a growing sense of hope carry the story across oceans, kingdoms, and curses.
A creeping escalation of supernatural threats—haunted homes, spirits, and demonic logic—where the family tries to survive the unseen.
Large-scale sci-fi action where a superhero team battles alien threats and escalating stakes across a connected cinematic universe.
Big, dumb momentum comedies where bad decisions turn into escalating road chaos and punchline payoffs.
Family-friendly fantasy adventures built on young protagonists, legendary worlds, and moral battles between good and evil.
Rom-coms where a social misfit collides with wealth and status, turning courtship into a comedy of manners.
Moody sci-fi futures where AI, androids, and surveillance noir collide with big existential questions.
Surreal fantasy survival stories where women fight to break impossible systems—often through stylized violence and escape attempts.
High-octane street races and heists fueled by loyalty, betrayal, and escalating personal grudges.
Close-up stories of young people navigating first love, friendship, and identity—often with quiet humor and sharp emotional edges.

description: Charm-first romances where letters, art, and travel set up misunderstandings, soft suspense, and unexpected connection.
Big-screen space missions that mix sci-fi spectacle, action set pieces, and ensemble character energy.
Island-sized creature showdowns with apes, dinosaurs, and nuclear-level spectacle.
plain_label: Kaiju monster brawls on the big scale

description: Bright, kid-friendly animation where a lovable cast tangles with an out-of-this-world threat and turns it into a friendship-and-hope victory lap.
Hard-edged crime films that track drug empires, moral collapse, and the grinding collision between cops, courts, and street logic.
Big-budget end-of-the-world thrillers where scientists and strangers scramble as meteors, storms, and climate-flavored disasters close in.
Stylish, darkly playful stories of monsters and misfits—often stop-motion or gothic animation—where melancholy turns into connection.
White-knuckle investigations and cat-and-mouse mind games centered on serial killers, psychological collapse, and forensic obsession.
Slow-burn dread where witchcraft, isolation, and family rot fester inside forests and old-world superstition.

description: Vampire-and-monster stories where Dracula’s world collides with hotels, family chaos, and punchline-friendly horror.
Colorful animated animal stories where big feelings and bigger schemes—often led by a charismatic villain—drive the laughs and quests.
Spy films that mix undercover tradecraft with sharp suspense or satire, trading glamour for paranoia and misdirection.

description: A fast-talking mix of undercover identity games and crime-adjacent chaos, usually powered by a sharp ensemble and comedic stakes.

description: Taut, human-stakes war dramas where intelligence work, raids, and real operations collide with moral pressure.
High-school romance driven by secret letters that accidentally turn longing into momentum.
Mismatched partners, escalating police absurdity, and crime plots treated like a live-action punchline.
Confessional, fashion-aware relationship stories where romantic misfires turn into loud, messy comedy and tender self-reinvention.
Killer-on-the-loose horror built on slasher mechanics—murders, remakes, and ramped-up gore with Halloween-season momentum.

description: High-tension mysteries where suspicion, infidelity, and missing pieces turn into a slow-motion psychological collapse.
Fast-talking buddy cops and undercover misadventures where the investigation is mostly a vehicle for escalating jokes and bad decisions.
Friend groups thrown into rule-bound fantasy simulations—jungles, dungeons, or alien basketball courts—where action and comedy collide.
Cerebral, character-driven heist stories where robberies become investigations and con artists play chess with the FBI and each other.
Bright, joke-forward animated stories where a lovable supervillain and a found-family crew turn chaos into heartfelt comedy.
A survival story where the apocalypse is the joke and the jokes are the survival plan.
Big landscapes, big wars, and intimate losses—history told through relationships and endurance.
A horror run on logic and cruelty—victims pushed through engineered trials where every move is a test.
Superpowers collide with alien possession energy—high-octane fights, shifting identities, and after-credits chaos.
A crime-soaked superhero world where training montages and secret identities get undercut by messier, darker jokes.
High-stakes trials where law collides with racism, power, and personal survival.
Comedy built on ghosts and monsters, where scares keep getting interrupted by punchlines.
Immortal longing and high-school stakes wrapped in vampire-and-werewolf melodrama.
Gloves-off missions through cartel territory where violence, corruption, and revenge drive the plot.
Big-sky science fiction where alien threats become fuel for satire, dystopia, and dark comedy.
Big-hearted animated sequels that sprint through action-comedy set pieces, then reward patience with after-credits stingers.

description: High-energy police showdowns where mismatched partners turn violence, banter, and procedure into momentum.

description: Dark crime stories where ordinary people or damaged agents spiral into revenge, coercion, and moral reckoning.
Understated sports dramas built around relentless training, mentorship, and the emotional calculus of a comeback fight.
High-stakes naval battles where alien threats, giant beasts, and wartime chaos collide in watery set pieces.
Fantasy adventures built around wizards, magical creatures, and episodic quests that feel like puzzles you can rob.
Stylish vampire-and-werewolf battles in a dark, modern world where fights are choreographed like mythology.
Big-spirited pirate journeys across exotic islands, where curses, rival crews, and bold swordplay drive the adventure.
High-octane crime thrillers where the hunt is relentless—FBI agents, prison breaks, and escalating shootouts turn every lead into danger.

description: Bright, storybook fantasy built around mischief, spells, and wonder that never loses its sense of play.
Space travel stories where the real tension comes from confinement, distance, and the psychological weight of a single mission.
High-velocity survival action where zombies, mutants, and lab-bred horror are tied to corporate plots and collapsing safe zones.
Tense horror-mysteries where visions of disaster force characters into increasingly doomed attempts to outsmart fate.
Teen-centered wizardry at a school that gradually turns from wonder into a high-stakes fight against dark magic.

description: Western stories where honor is a myth, violence has paperwork, and mercy arrives too late.
Fast, brainy investigations where sacred texts, secret societies, and murder clues spiral into paranoia.
Family-first adventures where animals (and the people who love them) carry the emotional weight.
Dramatic, real-event rescues and ship/sea catastrophes where leadership and grit are tested minute by minute.
Heart-tugging romances that start in hope and end in consequence—often adapted from novels.
Fast, bleak thrillers where a hard-edged protector hunts backroom criminals and “rescues” people the system won’t.
Sweeping, character-driven gangster stories where power grows, families fracture, and every deal tightens the noose.
Big, bright comedy-musicals where groups train, audition, and bicker their way through high-stakes singing competitions.
Science-fiction suspense where robots, android systems, or AI proxies reshape everyday life—and turn violence into a feature, not a bug.
Intimate, emotionally punishing dramas about love, marriage, infidelity, addiction, and the quiet devastation that follows.
Films where the summit becomes a countdown and survival depends on grit, weather, and hard choices.
Over-the-top horror parodies that treat jump scares like punchlines and franchises like a playground.
Stories where time travel or time-based technology reshapes love, identity, and the cost of changing the clock.
Hard-edged action war films where a veteran’s mission turns into a brutal, personal fight against overwhelming odds.
Dystopian futures where intelligent primates rise, forcing humans and apes into a heartbreaking cycle of war and survival.

description: Hard-edged, quippy mutant stories where lethal skills and messy morals collide.
Crime thrillers where the most dangerous force is a mind—often female—turning empathy into a weapon.
Big-budget battles of transforming machines, aliens, and human-scale heroes caught in robot wars.
Modern comedy-romances where dating misfires, one-night stands, and friendship fallout lead to unexpectedly tender lessons.
Movies where gaming worlds blur with real life—VR, dystopian platforms, and digital obsession driving the danger.
Spy thrillers where identity fractures and the hunt becomes a maze of flashes, surveillance, and sudden violence.
Smart, warm romantic comedies where big-city life (and seasonal chaos) turns awkward longing into heartfelt timing.
Young-adult science-fiction survival stories built around deadly systems—mazes, labs, and escalating dystopias—where escape is the only compass.
Comedy-fantasy buddy stories where a toy/mascot premise turns everyday life into chaotic, affectionate absurdity.

Teen stories where jokes land hard, feelings land harder, and growing up happens in public.
Stylish murder-mystery cases built like social puzzles—detectives, suspects, and sharp comedic misdirection.
Team-up superhero action where the mission is messy, the heroes are compromised, and the villains are never far off the leash.
Raw, character-first dramas where training, rivalry, and physical combat become a route to identity and redemption.
Spacefaring problem-solvers race the physics and the clock, where survival depends on science, grit, and a brutal mission timeline.
Big-cat royalty stories told through roar-loud musicality, family bonds, and the push-pull of legacy.

Epic, mythic space adventures where factions collide and heroism is written like legend.
Heart-forward films where athletic ambition becomes a fight for dignity, justice, or survival—based on real events.
High-gloss, globe-trotting spy adventures driven by secrets, surveillance, and escalating set-piece action.

Stories of Catholic institutions where possession, ritual, and doctrine collide with investigation, power, and conspiracy.
Giant-monster or alien disasters filtered through amateur footage energy, turning everyday people into witnesses of the impossible.
Post-apocalypse terror where survival hinges on controlling sound, fear, and family under constant predation.
Character-driven dramas that braid sexuality, ambition, and identity into a bruising portrait of modern life and artistic hunger.
Survival horror in extreme cold where an unknown presence turns isolation into a grotesque biological fight for time.
Bright, modern teen/early-adult romance comedies where crushes, insecurity, and social misfires collide into a hopeful love story.
Spectacle-heavy sci-fi adventures that treat alien worlds as emotional battlegrounds, with family bonds steering the action.
Dark mysteries where investigations spiral into obsession, dread, and moral questions under the pressure of relentless violence.
Light, fast superhero adventures where magic systems and family-like team dynamics fuel the jokes, action, and end-credits stingers.
Comedies where makeover fantasies—sometimes literal, sometimes psychological—turn vanity, romance, and humiliation into high-speed farce.
Big, loud action-adventure entries built around gaining or awakening superhuman power—often with reboot or continuity reset energy.
plain_label: Superhero origin blockbusters & rebooted power fantasies

description: Horror films where paranoia, grief, and family fractures spiral into ritualistic, mystery-driven terror.
Cold, sleek action-thrillers centered on assassins and hit squads, where missions collide with secret organizations, politics, and revenge.
plain_label: Assassin thriller with conspiracy & revenge hooks

description: Ensemble superhero stories about mutants—superhuman abilities, discrimination, and team dynamics—pushed through big action and shifting timelines.
